news.headline: ElevenLabs' AI platform now compliant with HIPAA, ensuring secure patient interactions in digital healthcare.
news.summary: The Conversational AI platform developed by ElevenLabs has recently attained compliance with HIPAA standards. This accomplishment helps guarantee the security of patient interactions and confidentiality of data within the digital healthcare realm.

news.headline: Chainalysis enhances DeFi capabilities by integrating with Ink, Kraken's Ethereum layer 2, offering automatic token support.
news.summary: In a recent development, Chainalysis has partnered with Ink, a DeFi-focused Ethereum layer 2 platform created by Kraken. This integration aims to bolster compliance and investigative processes by introducing automatic token support and advanced transaction monitoring capabilities. This collaboration is expected to streamline operations and enhance visibility for users.

news.headline: Korean investors flock to US leveraged ETFs amidst tech stocks fall.
Democrat lawmaker criticizes Trump's Bitcoin reserve plan, citing conflicts of interest. Calls for Treasury to halt it. #Cryptocurrency #Bitcoin #Trump
news.summary: House Representative Gerald E. Connolly criticized the US Treasury's plan to create a strategic crypto reserve, accusing President Trump of conflict of interest. He stated in a letter to Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent that the reserve would not benefit the American people and enrich Trump and his donors, calling it unsound fiscal policy. The White House clarified that the reserve would only hold forfeited crypto. Connolly alleged conflicts of interest with Trump's ownership of the crypto platform World Liberty Financial and the Trump memecoin, TRUMP. He requested documents related to the creation of the reserve and steps taken to avoid conflicts of interest. The reserve will use forfeited crypto and include other cryptocurrencies like XRP, Solana, Cardano, and Ether. Representative Maxine Waters also criticized the Trump memecoin as a "money grab."

news.headline: Solana's proposal to reduce inflation by up to 80% fails, with 74% staked supply voting. Approval fell short at 61.4%. Supporters praise governance process as a success.
news.summary: A proposal to change Solana's inflation system was rejected by stakeholders with only 43.6% in favor, falling short of the required 66.67%. The vote saw around 74% of the staked supply participating across 910 validators. The proposal, SIMD-228, aimed to shift from a fixed to a dynamic inflation model based on staking levels, with estimates suggesting an 80% reduction in inflation. Currently, Solana's inflation is at 4.66% with only 3% of the total supply staked. The proposed system aimed to adjust inflation to stabilize the network and encourage DeFi use. However, concerns were raised over complexity, smaller validators' profitability, and potential instability from fluctuating staking rates. Despite the rejection, stakeholders praised the governance process as a success, highlighting the significant participation and market cap involved. Solana's price saw a minimal dip of 1.5%, trading just below $125, as the network currently faces challenges following the burst of the memecoin bubble, leading to a 60% price decline in two months.

news.headline: Wall Street analysts revise Gogo's price expectations ahead of Q4 earnings release on March 14. Earnings estimated at 9 cents per share with projected revenue of $97.8 million. Gogo's acquisition of Satcom Direct completed on Dec. 4, 2024. Gogo shares closed at $6.87, down 1.2% on Thursday.
news.summary: Before the market opens on Friday, March 14, Gogo Inc. (NASDAQ:GOGO) is set to announce its fourth-quarter financial results. Analysts are anticipating earnings of 9 cents per share, a decline from the 11 cents per share reported in the same quarter last year. The company, based in Broomfield, Colorado, is expected to reveal quarterly revenue of $97.8 million, slightly lower than the $97.81 million recorded in the previous year. Gogo recently finalized its acquisition of Satcom Direct on Dec. 4, 2024, and appointed Chris Moore, the President of SD, as the new CEO. Following this news, Gogo's stock closed at $6.87 on Thursday, down by 1.2%. For more details, interested individuals can refer to Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Omneky introduces Smart Ads, using AI for ad creation. New feature promises high-performing, on-brand ads for marketers.
news.summary: In San Francisco, Omneky, known for its Gen AI command center for digital ads, has introduced Smart Ads, a groundbreaking feature aimed at revolutionizing the process of creating and optimizing ad content. By harnessing artificial intelligence, Smart Ads can produce top-performing, brand-appropriate ads automatically. This feature is designed to work seamlessly with a brand's guidelines, assets, and performance metrics. Marketers can now create fully designed and optimized ads with Smart Ads, a tool that empowers them to generate smarter ad content more efficiently.

news.headline: Sagicor Financial reports Q4 2024 results: Core earnings $28.0M, up 28% Y/Y, $90.9M for 2024, up 84% Y/Y. Dividend up 12.5%.
news.summary: Sagicor Financial Company Ltd. reported its results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2024. Highlights include core earnings to shareholders of $28.0 million for Q4 (up 28% Y/Y) and $90.9 million for the full year (up 84% Y/Y), core basic earnings per share of 20.4₵ for Q4 (up 33% Y/Y) and 64.9₵ for 2024 (up 87% Y/Y), core return on shareholders' equity of 11.7% for Q4 and 9.6% for 2024, net income to shareholders of $52.4 million for Q4, and $97.5 million for 2024. Shareholders' equity was at $959.7 million, with book value per share at US$7.08 or C$10.19, and a financial leverage ratio of 27.3%. The Group-LICAT ratio stood at 139%. The Board also approved a 12.5% increase in the quarterly dividend to US$0.0675 per common share. Sagicor provided an updated outlook and targets for 2025 and 2026, including core basic EPS expected to be 74.0₵ to 80.0₵ per share for 2025, and a targeted core return on shareholders' equity over the medium-term of 13%+. The company's business segments in Sagicor Canada, Sagicor Life USA, Sagicor Jamaica, and Sagicor Life had varying core earnings performances. For more detailed financial information, refer to the original source on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Analyst warns of Bitcoin trend shift if it drops below $74,000. Bitcoin gained nearly 390% from $16,625.08 to over $80,000.
news.summary: On a recent occasion, renowned cryptocurrency analyst Ali Martinez provided insights into potential price movements for Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC). Martinez emphasized the significance of closely monitoring the $74,000 level. Through a weekly Bitcoin-Tether (CRYPTO: USDT) chart analysis, he indicated that a breach of this crucial support level could signify a notable "potential trend shift," something not seen since January 2023. The analyst referenced historical data, noting that a sell signal was evident during the bearish trend in 2022, while a buy signal marked the initiation of a significant uptrend in early 2023, as illustrated in the chart provided. From January 2023 until now, Bitcoin has demonstrated an impressive surge from $16,625.08 to exceeding $80,000, reflecting a substantial gain of almost 390% over this period. Martinez's cautionary message to keep a close eye on the $74,000 level for Bitcoin was shared with followers and further details can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Vermont drops legal action against Coinbase for offering unregistered securities, following SEC’s lead. Multiple US states initially pursued Coinbase for staking without a license; Vermont’s decision prompts push for national legislation on crypto regulation. SEC dismisses cases post-Chair Gensler's resignation; Ripple Labs nears end of enforcement action. Request filed to reveal enforcement actions and taxpayer cost during Gensler's tenure. Elon Musk's blockchain government plan faces challenges.
news.summary: Vermont has decided to drop its legal action against Coinbase for allegedly offering unregistered securities to users through a staking service. The state's Department of Financial Regulation rescinded the "show cause order" after the US Securities and Exchange Commission dismissed its case on Feb. 28. The order had been filed on the same day that multiple US states launched legal proceedings against Coinbase in June 2023. Coinbase's chief legal officer praised Vermont's decision and called for comprehensive legislation to address the unique features of digital assets. A growing number of firms facing SEC enforcement actions have seen their cases dismissed following former SEC Chair Gary Gensler's resignation. Coinbase is seeking information on the number of enforcement actions brought against crypto firms during Gensler's tenure.

ADENTRA's 2024 results showed $2.18B in sales, with $530.8M in Q4 sales, $0.34 EPS, $42.2M EBITDA, and a 21.7% gross margin.
news.summary: ADENTRA Inc., a distributor of architectural building products in North America, reported financial results for Q4 2024, with sales of US$530.8 million, a 3.1% increase from Q4 2023. Full-year sales were $2.18 billion, a 2.5% decrease from 2023. The company's gross margin increased to 21.7% in 2024, with operating expenses rising to $377.2 million. Earnings per share were $1.95 in 2024, while adjusted earnings per share were $3.01. Adjusted EBITDA for 2024 was $184.3 million, with Q4 2024 seeing $42.2 million. The company generated $142.8 million in cash flow in 2024, acquired Woolf Distributing Company for $130 million, and raised dividends by 7%. Despite market headwinds in 2025, ADENTRA is positioned for stability due to its market presence, with limited tariff exposure. The company will hold an investor call on March 14, 2025, to discuss the results.

news.headline: Georgia legislature approves SB 144; Alliance commends move to protect farmers' access to crop protection tools.
news.summary: Today, Georgia lawmakers showed overwhelming bipartisan support by passing Senate Bill 144, a crucial legislation aimed at securing farmers' access to essential crop protection tools. This bold move sends a strong signal to states nationwide to take action. The bill's approval underscores the importance of supporting agriculture, with a focus on ensuring the continued ability to feed America.
